rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative three-component tandem synthesis of selenyl imidazo[2,1-b]thiazinones from diaryl diselenides, acryloyl chlorides, and 2-mercaptobenzimidazoles/2-mercaptoimidazoles.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diaryl diselenide (PhSeSePh) to a selenium-centered cationic radical that fragments into a selenium cation (PhSe+) and a selenium radical (PhSe•); cathode reduces H+ to H2.
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh to a radical cation that splits into PhSe+ and PhSe•; base-mediated condensation of acryloyl chloride with 2-mercaptobenzimidazole gives acyl-thioether D, which undergoes intramolecular cyclization to carbanion E; E is trapped by PhSe+ to give the selenylated product, while PhSe• auto-couples to regenerate diselenide and H+ is reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeded with only slight yield decrease (80%), suggesting non-radical main pathway.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product still formed in 70% yield; radical pathway not dominant.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product obtained in 67% yield; radical trapping ineffective at suppressing reaction.

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl (in place of PhSeSePh, no electricity)
probe_reagents.observation: Afforded 4a in 80% yield without current, supporting in situ generation of selenium cation.

probe_reagents.name: D2O (replacing diselenide)
probe_reagents.observation: Deuterated product 6-d obtained in 95% yield with 96% D-incorporation, supporting a carbanion intermediate E.

notes: Radical trapping experiments (TEMPO/BHT/DPE) did not suppress the reaction, leading the authors to favor an ionic (cation/anion) pathway over a radical chain; CV showed oxidation of 1a at 1.327 V supporting anodic activation of the diselenide. An alternative full-radical pathway (PhSe• addition) is implicitly disfavored but cannot be entirely excluded.
